figures.caption: Fig. 4: Sample results of Cooperative Foundational Models (CFM) and ours.

Fig. 1: Global-local feature imbalance in VLMs under cooperative closed-set and open-set detection. Existing methods (left) leverage VLMs for open-vocabulary detection while overlooking the VLMs’ limited capability in encoding regionlevel representations. Our approach (right) improves local adaptivity of VLMs without sacrificing the generalization capability.

Fig. 3: Overview of the proposed Decoupled Adaptivity Training (DAT). Using the region-award dataset, the pre-trained VLM is fine-tuned in a self-supervised and parameter-efficient manner, and the final backbone is integrated into the detection pipeline.
Table 1 Results on COCO. Our method shows new state-of-the-art results, particularly on novel categories.
Table 2 Open-vocabulary detection performance on LVIS v1.0 validation set. Our method outperforms existing approaches across all splits.

Fig. 2: The Cooperative Foundational Models (CFM) pipeline with the VLM (CLIP) visual backbone fine-tuned with our decoupled adaptivity training (DAT). CFM synergizes an auxiliary detector (Grounding DINO) with a closedset detector (Mask R-CNN), where its background proposed bounding boxes (b(BG)
